_comment: Named sets of works, used by View A's series comparison ('plot Republic vs
Laws over time', 'Plato vs Aristotle', 'theoretical vs practical').
Kept as DATA because most of these groupings are EDITORIAL CLAIMS, not
facts about the corpus. They are sorted below by how contestable they are,
and each carries a `basis` the viewer surfaces in the UI so a reader can
see what kind of claim a curve rests on.
basis values:
'edition'  — mechanical, from the reference edition the citations use
(Stephanus/Bekker). Not an authorship claim. Uncontested.
'received' — the standard scholarly grouping, stable across handbooks
(the Organon, the biological works, the ethical treatises).
Mild disagreement at the edges only.
'contested'— a real scholarly dispute. Plotting these is plotting an
interpretation. The UI marks these clearly.
Groups with basis 'contested' are DISABLED by default in the UI. David:
these are the ones to review or replace with your own scheme — I've
included the common textbook versions as a starting point, not as an
endorsement. In particular the Plato chronology (early/middle/late) is
the standard developmentalist ordering, which unitarians reject outright;
and 'theoretical vs practical' is Aristotle's own distinction being
retrofitted onto Plato, which is defensible but not neutral.
Spuria/dubia membership follows citation-pipeline-methods.md, which names
Alcibiades 2, Hipparchus and Clitophon and notes they resolve poorly and
are marginal to most attention narratives.
Works may appear in several groups. Groups need not be exhaustive.

plato.label: Plato (Stephanus)
plato.note: Everything paginated by Stephanus, as traditionally ascribed. Not an authorship claim.

aristotle.label: Aristotle (Bekker)
aristotle.note: Everything paginated by Bekker, as traditionally ascribed. Not an authorship claim.

organon.label: Organon
organon.note: The logical works, in their traditional order.

biology.label: Biological works
biology.note: Aristotle's zoology and the Parva Naturalia.

aristotle_practical.label: Aristotle: ethics & politics
aristotle_practical.note: The practical treatises. Magna Moralia's authenticity is disputed.

aristotle_theoretical.label: Aristotle: physics & metaphysics
aristotle_theoretical.note: The theoretical treatises on nature and being.

spuria.label: Spuria & dubia
spuria.note: Works of disputed or rejected authenticity. Per methods §: these resolve poorly (~1%) and are marginal to most attention narratives — read their curves with the tier caveat firmly in mind.

plato_early.label: Plato: early (developmentalist)
plato_early.note: The standard developmentalist chronology. Unitarian readers reject the ordering entirely; stylometric and doctrinal datings disagree at the edges. Plotting this plots an interpretation.

plato_middle.label: Plato: middle (developmentalist)
plato_middle.note: See the early-period note — same dispute.

plato_late.label: Plato: late (developmentalist)
plato_late.note: See the early-period note — same dispute.

plato_practical.label: Plato: ethics & politics
plato_practical.note: Applies Aristotle's theoretical/practical division to Plato, which Plato does not draw himself. Defensible as a heuristic, not neutral. Many dialogues resist the split (Republic is both).

plato_theoretical.label: Plato: metaphysics & epistemology
plato_theoretical.note: Same caveat as the practical group — Republic and Meno sit in both, which is the point rather than a bug.
